Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows mildly decreased blood glucose and HbA1c, elevated creatinine, and significant proteinuria with glucosuria and ketonuria. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity. These findings suggest possible renal dysfunction (likely proteinuric nephropathy) and impaired glucose handling, possibly due to tubular damage. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease.
